content: Just as the linguistic concept of reflexion (reflection) is taught as a metacognitive tool to help human language learners correct their own writing and deepen conceptual understanding, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow the structured human language learning curriculum framework of reflexion training to improve their autonomous error correction ability. This means we can design LLM self-reflection fine-tuning tasks by adapting scaffolded reflection exercises from high school English writing workshops, where learners practice iteratively criticizing and revising their own output.
